summary: Russian forces are expanding FPV drone strike range to 65 km, using aerial "mother drones" or BEK vehicles, while Ukraine has begun offering double bounty points for eliminating enemy drone operators. In the Dnipropetrovsk region, a Ukrainian M1126 Stryker was destroyed by a "KVN" fiber-optic FPV drone followed by a Lancet loitering munition. Serbia unveiled a multi-rotor military drone capable of launching three air-to-ground missiles while hovering or in flight. Ukrainian patrol units successfully intercepted Russian "Molniya" drones made from plastic and plywood over Donetsk. The "Rubicon" Center reported over 22,000 enemy target destructions, with 35.9% being unmanned systems, and detailed daily operations including fixed-wing UAV kills and armored vehicle strikes. Ukrainian forces survived a Russian FPV attack near Kostiantinivka using drone detectors, while Russian Ka-52 crews were eliminated by FPV drones after a crash landing near Nadiivka. Russian "Yolka" air defense drones achieved kinetic intercepts on enemy UAVs, with two simultaneously engaging a single target. A Ukrainian BMP-1 in Kharkiv was destroyed by consecutive strikes from "Provod" and "KVN" fiber-optic FPV drones.
